changes: Success confirmations can be turned off. The 'everything went fine' popups — Applied, Reverted, Cover proposed/applied, artist.jpg saved, Internet check finished — now carry a 'Don't show these confirmations again' checkbox, and there is a matching switch in Settings → General. When turned off, a successful action just posts a short note to the status bar instead of a popup you have to click away. Warnings and errors are never suppressed — anything that goes wrong is still shown, including an internet check with unmatched albums.
The Settings 'Writing' tab is renamed to 'General' and moved to the front — it holds general behavior (file times, confirmations, compilations name, history depth), not just writing options.

'Tag versions kept per file' now explains itself on hover: it is the History feature's depth — before every write the app snapshots all of the file's tags so History can show and revert to them; this number says how many versions are remembered per file before the oldest are deleted.

The online cover search now shows the pixel resolution of every result: after searching, each release in the list gets its cover's real size appended ('1500×1500 px') — or 'no cover' when the release has no image at all — so you can pick the largest one instead of guessing. Only the image headers are downloaded for this, so it stays fast.

Choosing a cover now embeds the FULL-RESOLUTION original from the Cover Art Archive. Previously the 500px preview thumbnail was what ended up in the tracks and folder.jpg, whatever the real size of the artwork; the preview is still the small thumbnail (fast), but 'Use this cover' downloads the original.

Discogs is available as a second cover source: enter a personal access token in Settings → Checks (free Discogs account → Settings → Developers → 'Generate new token') and 'Change cover…' lists Discogs results — with their resolutions — alongside MusicBrainz. Without a token the search works as before; Discogs only returns its images to authenticated requests, which is why the token is needed.
The cover preview shows the resolution of the previewed image underneath, including for images loaded from disk or dragged in.

The cover search now has TWO confirm buttons instead of one. 'Use this cover — save as proposal' is the old behavior: the cover is stored as a proposal and written together with the album's other changes on the next Apply. 'Use this cover — apply now' embeds the cover into all tracks (+ folder.jpg) immediately — and applies ONLY the cover: every other pending proposal of the album stays untouched, waiting for a normal Apply.

Applying a cover chosen in the search dialog now REPLACES an existing folder.jpg. Previously it obeyed the 'Overwrite existing folder.jpg' setting (off by default), so the new image went only into the MP3s while the old smaller folder.jpg survived — which immediately raised a 'folder image is smaller than the embedded cover' problem and needed a second apply to resolve. A cover you picked yourself is meant for both places, so folder.jpg is now written in the same apply.

Fixed 'folder image is smaller than the embedded cover' surviving the very apply that fixed it. Writing folder.jpg (from a chosen cover or from the embedded art) now refreshes the album's recorded folder-image size and re-checks the album's rules immediately, so the problem disappears right after applying instead of lingering until the next rescan.

The full-size cover viewer (click the cover thumbnail in the album view) has a new 'Change cover…' button next to −/+/Fit/Close: it closes the viewer and opens the cover search straight away. The album view's 'Find cover online…' button is renamed to 'Change cover…' to match — it always did more than searching online (load from disk, drag & drop).

The 'Re-encode text as UTF-8' check no longer fires forever on files edited in Mp3tag. ID3v2.4 stores the text encoding separately in every single frame, so one tag legitimately mixes them — and Mp3tag writes the purely numeric frames (track, disc, year) and the picture description as latin-1 even when it is set to write UTF-8, because for digits latin-1 and UTF-8 are the very same bytes. The app treated any such frame as damage, 'fixed' it, and the next Mp3tag edit brought it straight back: an endless ping-pong over something that changes no text at all. These frames are now exempt, while every real text frame (title, artist, album, comment, custom tags …) is still required to be UTF-8 — with or without accented characters. A numeric frame that does hold non-ASCII text after all (a genuinely damaged tag) is still reported.

Do a full re-read (Rescan on an album/artist, or 'Re-read all files') to clear this warning from files that were already scanned — the encoding is recorded when a file is read, so a quick scan, which only re-reads files whose size or date changed, will keep reporting the old result on untouched files.

Multi-line tag values (lyrics above all) are readable again in the track and album views. A table cell paints every line of its text but is only one line high, so a value with real line breaks came out with its lines drawn on top of each other. Such a value is now shown on ONE line with a visible ⏎ for each break — the file itself is not touched, the cell's tooltip shows the real multi-line text, and copying (Ctrl+C or the copy icon) still hands out the original with its line breaks intact.

Every tag in a file is now read, searchable and editable — not just the standard fields. Named comment frames (COMM:MusicMatch_Situation, COMM:Songs-DB_Occasion and the like), custom TXXX tags and embedded lyrics were previously invisible: they were never stored, so they could not be found in Search and could not be edited. They now appear as fields of their own — 'Comment [MusicMatch_Situation]', 'Custom [BARCODE]', 'Lyrics' — in the track and album views and in the Search field picker, and are matched by '(any field)' too. Editing one writes back into exactly that frame and leaves the other tags alone. Do a full re-read (Rescan, or 'Re-read all files') to pick these up on files that have not otherwise changed.

Search results can be grouped by album: tick 'Group by album' to collapse the matching tracks into one row per album, with the number of matching tracks — the list to work through when you want to fix whole albums rather than single tracks.

Double-clicking a search result now really opens it in the library: the artist and album are expanded and the track — or the whole album, in grouped mode — is selected in the catalog on the LEFT, so both sides of the window agree and the album can be edited and applied straight away. Previously only the right-hand panel changed while the tree kept its old selection. A hit hidden by the severity filter is revealed by widening the filter instead of being lost.
Coming back to Search keeps your conditions, your results and the row you last opened, so a long result list can be worked through album by album.

The 'Matched' column of the search results now actually says what was found: 'field: value' for every field a condition hit on. Previously it stayed empty for '(any field)' searches (the commonest kind) — it only ever filled itself when a condition named a field explicitly — and when it did show something, it listed the field even if that condition had NOT matched. Now an '(any field)' hit names the field the value was found in, a condition that failed inside an OR group is no longer reported as a match, and an 'is empty' search shows the field as '(empty)'. Long values (lyrics, fingerprints) are shortened, with the full text in the cell's tooltip.

APEv2 tags that hold only technical data (ReplayGain / MP3Gain, no title/artist/…) are now detected and offered for removal like any other foreign APEv2 block. Previously such a tag was invisible to the app even though other tools (e.g. Mp3tag) reported it was still there. The complete tag is now snapshotted, so a removed ReplayGain-only APEv2 tag is fully restored by History revert. Do a full re-read (Rescan, or 'Re-read all files') to pick these up on files that have not otherwise changed.

Embed folder image as cover art: when a track has no embedded cover but the album folder has a folder.jpg / cover.jpg / …, the app now proposes embedding it. When both exist but differ in size, it keeps the higher-resolution one — embedding the folder image when it is larger, or refreshing folder.jpg from the embedded cover when the embedded art is larger. Controlled by a new 'Embed folder image' option in Settings → Checks.

Whole deleted folders are now reconciled: an 'All folders in the root' scan also covers folders that were removed from disk since the last scan, so their tracks turn gray (missing) — or are removed when 'Automatically remove whole gone albums' is ticked. Previously a fully deleted top-level folder was invisible to the scan and lingered as if still present.

APEv2 / ID3v1 conflicts are easier to read: a field you decided to keep at ID3v2 now shows a green '✓ keeping ID3v2' marker, while fields still awaiting a decision show 'decide me'. APEv2 rows also label which value is the current ID3v2 one. Fixes a case where an APEv2 alternative value was mistaken for the real tag, spawning bogus 'artist should hold multiple values' and 'artist should include the album artist' warnings.

Album view header redesigned: the first line now reads 'Album artist — Album (Year)', and the second line shows the full folder path with the Open-folder button beside it. The album cover thumbnail is aligned with the fields table and shows its pixel size underneath.
Cover viewer improvements: the mouse wheel now zooms toward the pointer and only zooms (never scrolls), and you can pan a zoomed-in image by dragging it with the left mouse button.

Missing files: MP3s that were deleted or moved on disk no longer vanish silently. They now show a gray marker that propagates up to the album and artist, a new 'Missing files (gray)' filter narrows to them, and clicking one offers to remove just that leftover entry. A scan option can automatically remove whole gone albums (whole folder missing), while albums that lost only some tracks are kept and marked gray.

ID3v1 / APEv2 conflicts are now decided per field and are fully reversible: 'Use the old tag's value' and 'Keep ID3v2' act only on the rows you selected, you can choose differently for each field, and you can switch any field back at any time. The foreign tag is only removed once every differing field is decided (a per-rule option can allow removing it anyway). Fixes a bug where 'Keep ID3v2' discarded conflict options you had not selected.

Copy from the metadata tables: the value in the Current column is now selectable text — select part of a value and press Ctrl+C — with a one-click copy icon revealed on hover. Clicking a value no longer highlights the whole cell.

Album cover viewer: click the album cover to open it at full resolution in a floating window with mouse-wheel and +/- zoom, fit-to-window (never overflowing the screen), the pixel resolution and zoom shown at the bottom, and a Close button.
User data (config.json, themes.json, library databases) can be redirected with the MP3TAGGER_DATA_DIR environment variable, keeping automated tests fully separated from real settings.

APEv2 tags: MP3s that carry a foreign APEv2 tag (appended by some old rippers and by MP3Gain) are now detected and handled exactly like a leftover ID3v1 tag. The APEv2 block is checked against ID3v2 — if every metadata value matches, its removal is proposed; if a value differs, a conflict is shown and you can either copy the APEv2 value into ID3v2 or keep the ID3v2 value; a value found only in APEv2 is rescued into ID3v2. The tag is removed in the same write that resolves it.
New 'Remove foreign APEv2 tags' option (Settings → Problem types, Options column) mirrors the ID3v1 switch — off keeps the APEv2 block untouched. History revert restores a removed APEv2 tag.
The album/artist 'Rescan' button now always does a full re-read (every file, even unchanged ones) so newly added checks like APEv2 are picked up without needing a full library scan.

Scan log window: after every scan a copyable log shows problems first (unreadable files, unsafe file names — with full paths), then files not found on disk (renamed/moved/deleted) with a one-click option to remove their database entries or keep them.

Leftover entries of renamed/deleted files no longer appear as work to do: the change-type tree, counts and severity dots skip them, and opening such an album explains the situation instead of showing an empty editor with '0 tracks'.

Stable problem-type order: the change-type tree is sorted by severity and a fixed workflow priority (ID3v1 rescue → encoding → text repairs → field fixes → images) — the order no longer shuffles after applying changes.
Track view: huge unbreakable custom-tag values (e.g. AcoustID fingerprints) are shortened for display — they can no longer stretch the window to absurd widths.
Titles containing < > characters display correctly in the headers.

ID3v1/ID3v2 conflict offers keep their current/proposed values: other rules no longer mistake the offered old-v1 value for the real tag (e.g. 'Kiioto' vs 'Kiiōtō' no longer spawns a bogus artist proposal that displaced the conflict offer).
Clear buttons: remove a field's existing value — per album (fields table) or per track (track view). Clearing proposes 'current value → nothing'; applying it deletes the tag from the MP3.
Clear and typed values replace each other: after Clear you can still type a replacement value (and vice versa) before applying.
Removal proposals are now clearly shown as '‹remove value›' in all change lists.
Emptying a proposed-value cell now consistently withdraws the proposal (in the album boxes, the album change list and the track view).
Auto-update: the app checks GitHub for new versions at startup (can be turned off) and offers to install them.
New 'Updates' tab in Settings: installed version, manual update check, what's new in each version, one-click update.
Updating downloads the new version, installs it and restarts the app; your settings, libraries and databases are never touched.
Initial release: library scanning, rule engine, review-and-apply workflow, tag history with revert, multi-condition search, online metadata on demand, themes and translatable field labels.
